hard.next_step: June'26 : Send the Value Engineering template and NDA to Anna to support her internal business case. Reconnect in September to review budget status, discuss the findings, and plan the next workshop with key stakeholders.

north_star_verdict.headline: The 26 Jun 2026 risk-focused demo with Anna Gortz landed strong product-market fit and a 1 Jul 2026 follow-up kept it warm, but the 26 Jan 2027 close date is a placeholder: a deliberate 3-month summer silence until a September reconnect, a single-threaded Evaluator with no budget owner mapped, and a full enterprise procurement motion (RFI/RFP, shortlisting, use-case validation, commercials, EB approval, contracting) all still ahead.
north_star_verdict.math: Today 6 Jul 2026. Created 29 Jun 2026 (7 days ago), qualified same day, so 7 days in Qualified. Discovery 12 Jun, demo 26 Jun, follow-up email 1 Jul 2026. Close 26 Jan 2027 is 204 days out, but the planned July-August silence with a September reconnect consumes ~75 of those days before work resumes, leaving ~120 days for budget validation, multi-threading, RFI/RFP, shortlisting, use-case demos, commercials, EB approval and contracting. A typical 12-15 month enterprise cycle from first discovery anchors a realistic close in mid-2027, not January. No forward slip is recorded yet; the deal is healthy and engaged and the sole problem is the date.
evidence: Demo 26 Jun 2026 with Anna Gortz established strong product-market fit: continuous supplier risk monitoring, bulk ERP imports for acquisitions, integrated third-party risk data (financial, ESG, cyber, sanctions), combined SRPM plus sourcing analytics, AI-assisted supplier data mapping. Anna: 'Demo aligns with needs; primary focus is getting timely, continuous supplier risk visibility for the division.' (Avoma notes 26 Jun 2026, carried forward)

Rep follow-up on 1 Jul 2026 (Clari email, logged Task): 'Thank you for your time the other day, it was a great session' after the 26 Jun risk-focused demo. This is a rep-side touch awaiting Anna's reply, not fresh buyer engagement.

Next_Step__c: 'Send the Value Engineering template and NDA to Anna to support her internal business case. Reconnect in September to review budget status, discuss the findings, and plan the next workshop with key stakeholders.' A deliberate 3-month silence through July-August, reconnect September 2026.

Single contact (Anna Gortz, Supplier Assurance Director, Evaluator role), no Economic Buyer or Decision Maker mapped, budget authority unconfirmed. Anna expects follow-up discussions after summer (September) to pursue procurement/risk-tool procurement and design. (Avoma notes 26 Jun 2026)
The $15,000 amount and 26 Jan 2027 close are placeholders, not business-driven: no compelling event, audit deadline or fiscal date has been stated to force the timeline.

metrics.narrative: On the 26 Jun 2026 risk-focused demo, Anna Gortz articulated a clear operational pain: moving her decentralized Global Solutions division from historical, backward-looking supplier risk analysis to continuous, up-to-date monitoring, with bulk upload and automated mapping of acquired companies' supplier data during due diligence, and combining iRisk (SRPM) with sourcing, contracting and spend analytics to assess revenue and EBIT at risk by supplier or entity. No quantified ROI, savings target, headcount or cycle-time number was tied to the project. Anna referenced internal cost-saving initiatives ('cost saving initiatives on a, say, 50% on a yearly basis on the items we are buying') but attached no figure to this investment. Quantifying the value case is the immediate step, which is exactly why she requested the Value Engineering template.
sources: Avoma call 26 Jun 2026, Anna Gortz: 'A central, continuously monitored supplier risk solution is required to move from historical to up-to-date supplier risk visibility across the division.'
Avoma call 26 Jun 2026, Anna Gortz: 'Bulk upload and automated mapping of acquired companies supplier data (ERP imports) is needed so acquisitions supplier risk can be assessed during due diligence.'

Avoma call 26 Jun 2026, Anna Gortz: 'Combining SRPM (iRisk) with sourcing, contracting and spend analytics will provide stronger analytics, scorecards and the ability to assess revenue/EBIT at risk by supplier or entity.'
Next_Step__c: 'Send the Value Engineering template and NDA to Anna to support her internal business case.'

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named in any source. Anna Gortz (Supplier Assurance Director) holds the Evaluator role and is explicitly seeking, not granting, budget: on 26 Jun 2026 she said next steps depend on internal budgeting and expects to review budget status only after the summer (September). The Next_Step confirms the reconnect is to 'review budget status,' which shows the money sits above Anna with a person she has not yet named or introduced. There is no OpportunityContactRole, task contact or call reference identifying who owns the budget for a divisional supplier-risk investment. The move is to have Anna name the budget owner and secure an introduction; until then the deal cannot advance past business-case building.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are emerging from the demo, not from a formal RFP or BRD yet. On 26 Jun 2026 Anna Gortz validated a specific feature set as her yardstick: continuous (versus point-in-time) supplier risk monitoring, integrated third-party risk data spanning financial, ESG, cyber and sanctions, bulk ERP import and AI-assisted mapping so newly acquired companies can be risk-assessed during due diligence, and the ability to combine iRisk supplier risk with sourcing and spend analytics for revenue/EBIT-at-risk scoring. These are confirmed as needs because the demo was oriented to them and Anna said it 'aligns with needs.' What is still inferred is the weighting, any must-have integration list, and whether IT or procurement will add technical or commercial criteria; those surface once she brings other stakeholders into the September workshop.
sources: Avoma call 26 Jun 2026, Anna Gortz: 'Demo aligns with needs; primary focus is getting timely, continuous supplier risk visibility for the division.'
Avoma call 26 Jun 2026: risk-focused demo covered financial, ESG, cyber and sanctions third-party risk data plus combined SRPM and sourcing analytics.

decision_process.narrative: Read from the call sequence, ASSA ABLOY is running an informal early-stage evaluation, not a formal procurement yet. A discovery call on 12 Jun 2026 was followed by a risk-focused demo on 26 Jun 2026, both with Anna Gortz as the sole buyer participant. The process Anna described for the next phase is: she builds an internal business case over the summer using the Value Engineering template, then reconnects in September to review budget status and 'plan the next workshop with key stakeholders.' That workshop is where the process becomes multi-party and where the real approval chain will first appear. No formal RFP, procurement gate, or committee has been named, and no approval steps are documented. Getting the intended evaluation and approval sequence stated in writing at the September reconnect is the way to make this concrete.
sources: SF Activities: Discovery call 12 Jun 2026; S2C Demo (Risk Focused) 26 Jun 2026 (both events logged).
Next_Step__c: 'Reconnect in September to review budget status, discuss the findings, and plan the next workshop with key stakeholders.'

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are entirely unknown at this Qualified stage, which is normal this early. The only paper artifact in play is an NDA, requested so Anna Gortz can share internal cost figures back on the Value Engineering worksheet, not a commercial contract. No MSA, Order Form, SOW, legal, InfoSec or procurement-channel discussion has occurred, and no current-contract expiration exists to act as a forcing function (this appears to be a greenfield supplier-risk initiative, not a renewal). Visibility will only come once Anna confirms budget and the buying process in September; the near-term paper step is simply getting the NDA executed so numbers can flow into the business case.

No MSA/SOW/InfoSec/procurement evidence in any field, task or call.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is confirmed and owned by Anna Gortz, Supplier Assurance Director for a decentralized division. On 26 Jun 2026 she described managing risk and supplier assurance across business units that lack consistent processes and data quality, with acute difficulty obtaining supplier data from newly acquired companies that lack proper ERP systems. The core pain is that supplier risk visibility today is historical and fragmented rather than central and continuous, and that acquisitions cannot be risk-assessed quickly during due diligence. This is a real operational and compliance exposure for the division, and Anna is the pain owner driving to solve it; the gap is that the financial magnitude of the pain has not yet been quantified.
sources: Avoma call 26 Jun 2026, Anna Gortz: 'A central, continuously monitored supplier risk solution is required to move from historical to up-to-date supplier risk visibility across the division.'
Avoma call 26 Jun 2026, Anna Gortz: challenges with data quality and difficulties obtaining data from acquisitions lacking proper ERP systems.
Avoma call 26 Jun 2026, Anna Gortz: bulk upload and automated mapping of acquired companies supplier data is needed so acquisitions supplier risk can be assessed during due diligence.

champion.narrative: Anna Gortz (Supplier Assurance Director, Evaluator role) is a developing champion, engaged and proactive but not yet proven to hold power. Advocacy she has shown: she engaged deeply through the 12 Jun discovery and the 26 Jun demo, articulated detailed pain and requirements, and committed to build an internal business case over the summer using the Value Engineering template, meaning she will carry Zycus forward internally. What is still missing is decisive: she has not named or introduced the economic buyer, has brought no other stakeholders (IT, procurement, senior supply-chain leadership) to the table, and is seeking budget approval rather than controlling it. Her strength gets tested at the September reconnect: if she returns with a completed business case, a named budget owner and stakeholder introductions, she is a strong champion; if she returns alone with budget still unclear, she is a coach without power and the deal stalls.
sources: Avoma call 26 Jun 2026: Anna Gortz led the demo and engaged deeply on functional-fit questions.
Next_Step__c: 'Reconnect in September to review budget status, discuss the findings, and plan the next workshop with key stakeholders.'

Single contact; no introductions to other stakeholders yet; budget authority unmapped.

competition.narrative: No competitor is named in any source: Competitors__c is blank, and Anna Gortz named no other vendor across the 12 Jun discovery and 26 Jun demo. This is a genuine knowledge gap, not evidence that Zycus is sole-sourced. Because Anna is still building an internal business case before any formal vendor comparison, ASSA ABLOY may be in early exploration, may have an incumbent supplier-risk or TPRM tool in place, or the strongest alternative may simply be doing nothing and continuing to manage supplier risk manually. The first job at the September reconnect is to surface current-state tooling and whether any parallel evaluation exists. If a formal process runs, rivals such as SAP Ariba, Coupa, Ivalua, GEP or specialist TPRM tools (Prevalent, Aravo, Venminder) will appear at RFI/RFP; the Zycus wedge is integrated Source-to-Contract plus iRisk SRPM plus Merlin AI-assisted supplier mapping, which point TPRM tools cannot match on sourcing and spend context.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor identified in any source as of 26 Jun 2026. The competitive landscape is genuinely unknown: it is unclear whether ASSA ABLOY runs an incumbent supplier-risk platform, evaluates vendors in parallel, or continues to manage risk manually (a do-nothing / status-quo alternative is plausible while Anna Gortz is still building the business case). This is a knowledge gap to close at the September reconnect, not a sign we are sole-sourced. If a formal evaluation emerges the wedge is integrated Source-to-Contract plus iRisk SRPM plus Merlin AI-assisted supplier mapping versus point TPRM tools that lack sourcing and spend context.

best_practice_check.summary: Early Qualified deal with strong product fit but three coverage levers that decide whether the September reconnect advances or stalls.
flags: Map the money before September: Anna Gortz is an Evaluator seeking budget, so get her to name and introduce the budget owner or the reconnect stalls at business-case stage.
Close the competitive blind spot: surface current supplier-risk tooling and any parallel evaluation, since do-nothing/manual is the most likely alternative while the case is being built.

Justify the timing and value: help Anna quantify the cost of fragmented, historical risk visibility in the Value Engineering case, and anchor a compelling event (audit or acquisition due-diligence) to make the Jan 2027 date real.

champion_strength.summary: Anna Gortz is engaged and proactive (led the demo, articulated pain, requested the Value Engineering template, committed to build the business case over summer and reconnect in September), but her strength is unproven. She holds the Evaluator role, not budget power, and has not yet introduced Zycus to senior leadership, IT, procurement or finance. The September reconnect is the test: return with budget clarity, a completed case and stakeholder introductions and she is strong; return alone with budget still unclear and she is a coach without power.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS is Strong and the demo surfaced concrete AI value (Merlin AI-assisted supplier data mapping and automated onboarding for acquired companies), but the AI story is not yet quantified or put in front of a budget owner. The Merlin agentic narrative is a differentiator worth foregrounding in the September value workshop.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Status is Strong and the 26 Jun 2026 demo corroborates real AI appetite: Anna Gortz engaged on AI-assisted supplier data mapping and automated onboarding of acquired-company data, which maps directly to Merlin. The field and the call agree.

Single-threaded on one Evaluator; no IT, procurement or senior supply-chain stakeholder engaged, leaving the deal exposed if Anna's internal case does not land.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is warm but entering a deliberate, buyer-agreed 3-month pause; the 1 Jul 2026 rep follow-up awaits a reply. Not cooling in a worrying sense, but momentum is intentionally parked until September.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is the well-fit early front-runner on a divisional supplier-risk initiative after a strong 26 Jun 2026 risk demo with Anna Gortz, but the deal is single-threaded on an Evaluator with no budget owner mapped and is heading into a buyer-agreed 3-month silence until a September budget review, so the only thing between here and a real deal is turning Anna's internal business case into a named, funded economic buyer.

win_position.text: Strong early position: the 26 Jun 2026 risk-focused demo aligned tightly with Anna Gortz's needs (continuous supplier risk monitoring, bulk ERP import and AI-assisted mapping for acquisitions), she called it 'a great session,' and AIS is Strong, so product fit is a genuine strength.

But no economic buyer or budget owner is identified: Anna is an Evaluator who said next steps 'depend on internal budgeting,' so the deal cannot advance until she names and introduces whoever controls the money in September.

And the deal is single-threaded with a completely unknown competitive picture, where the most likely alternative is the buyer continuing to manage supplier risk manually while the business case is being built.

deal_momentum.text: Two buyer touches in three weeks (12 Jun discovery, 26 Jun demo) plus a 1 Jul 2026 rep follow-up show real early cadence after the opp was created and qualified on 29 Jun 2026.
Momentum is deliberately parked: the buyer-agreed plan is a 3-month silence through July-August with a September reconnect, so no forward motion is expected for roughly the next 75 days.
The buyer named no concrete decision date and no compelling event, so the September reconnect is the only scheduled milestone and it depends on Anna's internal progress, not a fixed clock.

customer_commitment.text: Anna Gortz committed to build the internal business case herself over the summer and requested the Value Engineering template and NDA, which is a real ownership signal from an engaged evaluator.
Commitment stops at the evaluator line: no budget owner, decision maker or additional stakeholder has been introduced, and the September 'workshop with key stakeholders' is still only a plan.

deal_risk.text: The $15,000 amount and 26 Jan 2027 close are placeholders with no business-driven deadline, so the forecast date will almost certainly slip toward mid-2027 given the full enterprise motion still ahead.
Single-thread risk: if Anna's internal case fails to win budget over the summer, there is no second relationship to keep the deal alive, and the competitive/current-state picture is still a blank.

customer_preference.evidence: Deep Zycus-specific engagement and strong fit on the 26 Jun demo, but no explicit preference versus a named rival (none surfaced).

economic_buyer.evidence: No EB or budget owner identified; Anna is an Evaluator seeking approval.

momentum.evidence: Good early cadence but a buyer-agreed 3-month silence parks momentum until September.

business_case.evidence: Clear qualitative pain (fragmented, historical supplier-risk visibility across a decentralized, acquisitive division) but no quantified ROI or savings; Anna requested the Value Engineering template to build the numbers.
